div.h1: Ionosense HPC D2 Diagram Color Scheme

ul.li: Core Blue (#E6F3FF / #4169E1): Core platform, executors, C++ architecture
Execution Red (#FFE6E6 / #C00000): Execution layer, workflow phases
GPU Gold (#FFD700 / #B8860B): Device memory, GPU resources
Kernel Orange (#FFE4B5 / #FF8C00): CUDA kernels, C++ extension
Use core-blue for primary architectural components
Use streaming-green for continuous/streaming data flows
Use benchmark-orange for performance measurement components
Use note-gold for informational callouts and highlights
Use gpu-memory for all device memory allocations
Use host-memory for page-locked host buffers
Use pcie-transfer for explicit H2D/D2H transfer nodes
dependency: Dashed line (stroke-dash: 3) for loose coupling
implementation: Dashed + triangle for interface implementation
data-flow: Solid line with standard arrow for data movement
text-note: Italic, small font, no border (metadata)
section-header: Bold, medium font, no border (section titles)
text-label: Standard text, no border (inline labels)
Host: Input/output buffers + 2 pinned staging buffers
Device: 2 input buffers + intermediate + output + window + work area
No ring buffers: Direct pipeline flow
Host: Input/output buffers + 2 pinned staging buffers + ring buffers (98 KB) + batch staging (33 KB)
Ring buffers: Per-channel accumulation (3× NFFT capacity)
Use case: Real-time streaming with arbitrary chunk sizes

div.p: Both modes use the same device memory layout and processing pipeline. The streaming overhead is entirely in host memory for ring buffer management.
StreamingExecutor Mode: Low-latency streaming, arbitrary chunk sizes Total Memory: ~295 KB (nfft=2048, channels=2) +80% vs BatchExecutor

ul.li: Ring buffers: 3 × nfft × channels × sizeof(float) = 98 KB (extra)
Staging buffer: nfft × channels × sizeof(float) = 33 KB (extra)
Device buffers: same as BatchExecutor = 164 KB

div.p: Tradeoffs: ✅ Supports arbitrary input chunk sizes ✅ Continuous streaming capability ✅ Per-channel independence ❌ +80% memory overhead ❌ +40% latency (122.25 µs vs 86.79 µs)

ol.li: can_extract(nfft) - Check if full frame available
advance(hop_size) - Move read pointer for overlap

ul.li: Allows safe accumulation before first extraction
note: Extracted frames from ring buffers
